model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Duke is the clear pick because this is an extreme mismatch in both predictive quality and two-way efficiency: the Blue Devils are No. 1 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik, while Siena sits around the 190 range. Duke’s elite offense and top-tier defense give Siena very little margin to exploit, and the market view also reflects that gap with Duke installed as roughly a 30-point favorite. Siena’s credible path would require Duke to be unusually sloppy or limited by health, but the available reporting did not surface a major enough availability issue to justify fading the top overall seed.

reasoningStep.title: Ratings gap and health check strongly favor Duke
reasoningStep.summary: I compared Duke and Siena in combined KenPom/Torvik ratings and checked recent news for injuries or availability concerns. The statistical gap is massive on both offense and defense, and I did not find a major late-breaking health issue strong enough to materially change the matchup outlook.
evidence: KenPom: Duke No. 1 with +39.17 adjusted efficiency margin, 127.9 adjusted offense and 88.7 adjusted defense; Siena No. 192 with -2.11, 107.2 offense and 109.3 defense.
Torvik: Duke No. 1 with 128.0 adjusted offense and 90.4 adjusted defense; Siena No. 187 with 107.2 adjusted offense and 109.0 adjusted defense.
Recent web results and matchup previews indicate Duke is about a 29.5-point favorite, consistent with a dominant 1-vs-16 projection.
News searches surfaced some Duke health-question coverage, but no clear evidence of a major absence that would make Siena the better pick.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026

Current game label: East 1/16
Round: Round of 64
